overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from a sci-fi survival horror game featuring an astronaut exploring a space station. The character traverses dark industrial maintenance tunnels before entering a lush hydroponics bay with grow lights, examining vegetables and scientific equipment. Cinematic rendering with dramatic lighting shifts from red emergency lights to green grow lights.

segments.scene_description: The character emerges from the dark tunnel into a bright hydroponics bay filled with lush vegetation under artificial grow lights. The environment shifts from industrial metal to organic plant life with mist humidity systems visible among the growing towers.

segments.scene_description: The character moves through the greenhouse to a produce processing area, stopping to interact with crates of cucumbers and apples. The character picks up and examines the vegetables closely before continuing through the hydroponics facility.

segments.scene_description: The character enters a well-equipped laboratory workspace with hydroponic growing trays and a central workbench. The character interacts with plant samples and examines scientific equipment including a branded Terra case and various instruments.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous journey from the oppressive atmosphere of industrial maintenance corridors into the vibrant, sterile environment of a scientific hydroponics facility. It begins in a narrow corridor bathed in red emergency lighting, which shifts to yellow as the camera passes through a maintenance access door and into a dark tunnel characterized by metal grates and exposed red piping. As the character emerges from the tunnel, the scene opens abruptly into a bright hydroponics bay where industrial metal gives way to lush vegetation and misting humidity systems under artificial grow lights. The camera then moves through the greenhouse to a produce processing area to examine crates of cucumbers and apples before transitioning into a laboratory corridor featuring a large glass bee enclosure. Finally, the movement concludes inside a well-equipped laboratory workspace, shifting focus toward hydroponic growing trays, a central workbench, and various scientific instruments.
